name: Miriam Goldfarb
bio: Illuminated manuscripts fused with Jungian symbolism. Her Sefirot diagrams blend Kabbalistic structure with depth-psychological mapping.

name: Daniel Rosenfeld
bio: Sacred geometry and Hebrew letterforms as archetypal language. Trained at Betzalel Academy of Arts in Jerusalem.

name: Leah Sternberg
bio: Jungian analyst turned painter. Her Shadow series treats the Qliphoth as visual psychology — integration through image.

name: Cyrille Beaumont
bio: Alchemical illustrator working in the tradition of Michael Maier and the Rosicrucians. Ink and gold leaf on vellum.

name: Rachel Ben-David
bio: Mandala painter whose circular compositions map individuation through archetypal symbols across multiple traditions.

name: Amos Shapiro
bio: Printmaker combining etching with Lurianic cosmology — tzimtzum, shevirat ha-kelim, and tikkun as visual metaphors.

name: Naomi Blum
bio: Mixed media explorations of the divine feminine across traditions — Shekinah, Sophia, Isis, and Shakti as one luminous presence.

name: Tariq Mansour
bio: Sufi-inspired geometric abstraction. The 99 names of the divine dissolved into pattern. Calligraphy as cosmic motion.
